pattern: Merge the lists and sweep a min-heap that always holds one element per list, tracking the running maximum so each pop tests the tightest range covering all lists.
hints: A valid range must contain at least one value from every list, so think about the current minimum across lists versus the current maximum.
Use a min-heap seeded with the first element of each list plus the overall maximum among them; the window [heap-min, max] is a candidate.

Pop the smallest element, update the candidate range if it is tighter, then push the next element from that same list and refresh the max. Stop when any list is exhausted. Apply the tie-break: smaller width wins, then smaller start.

edge_inputs.description: second statement example, all identical lists
single list, range is just one element
two lists each a single value
tie in width resolved by smaller start

stress.description: several sorted lists of random integers
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 30)))
    nums = []
    for _ in range(k):
        m = rng.randint(1, 20)
        lst = sorted(rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(m))
        nums.append(lst)
    return [nums]
stress.note: each inner list is non-decreasing as required
